2605.12243.title: PreScam: A Benchmark for Predicting Scam Progression from Early Conversations
2605.12243.paper_summary: Conversational scams, such as romance and investment scams, are emerging as a major form of online fraud. Unlike one-shot scam lures such as fake lottery or unpaid toll messages, they unfold through multi-turn conversations in which scammers gradually manipulate victims using evolving psychological techniques. However, existing research mainly focuses on static scam detection or synthetic scams, leaving open whether language models can understand how real-world scams progress over time. We introduce PreScam, a benchmark for modeling scam progression from early conversations. Built from user-submitted scam reports, PreScam filters and structures 177,989 raw reports into 11,573 conversational scam instances spanning 20 scam categories. Each instance is hierarchically structured according to the scam lifecycle defined by the proposed scam kill chain, and further annotated at the turn level with scammer psychological actions and victim responses. We benchmark models on two tasks: real-time termination prediction, which estimates whether a conversation is approaching the termination stage, and scammer action prediction, which forecasts the scammer's subsequent actions. Results show a clear gap between surface-level fluency and progression modeling: supervised encoders substantially outperform zero-shot LLMs on real-time termination prediction, while next-action prediction remains only moderately successful even for strong LLMs. Taken together, these results show that current models can capture some scam-related cues, yet still struggle to track how risk escalates and how manipulation unfolds across turns.
2605.12243.paper_summary_zh: 對話詐騙，如戀愛詐騙和投資詐騙，正逐漸成為一種主要的網路詐騙形式。<lb/>與一次性詐騙誘餌（如假彩票或未付款的通行費訊息）不同，這些詐騙透過多輪對話展開，詐騙者逐步利用不斷演變的心理技術操控受害者。<lb/>然而，現有研究主要集中在靜態詐騙檢測或合成詐騙上，尚未探討語言模型是否能理解現實世界詐騙隨時間的進展。<lb/>我們介紹了PreScam，一個用於建模詐騙進展的基準，基於早期對話。<lb/>PreScam由用戶提交的詐騙報告構建，過濾並結構化177,989份原始報告，形成11,573個跨越20個詐騙類別的對話詐騙實例。<lb/>每個實例根據所提出的詐騙殺鏈定義的詐騙生命週期進行層次結構化，並在回合層面進一步註釋詐騙者的心理行為和受害者的反應。<lb/>我們在兩個任務上對模型進行基準測試：實時終止預測，估計對話是否接近終止階段，以及詐騙者行動預測，預測詐騙者的後續行動。<lb/>結果顯示，表面流暢性和進展建模之間存在明顯差距：監督編碼器在實時終止預測上顯著優於零樣本LLM，而即使對於強大的LLM，下一步行動預測的成功率仍然僅為中等。<lb/>綜合來看，這些結果顯示當前模型能捕捉到一些與詐騙相關的線索，但仍然難以追蹤風險如何升高以及操控如何在回合中展開。
2605.12243.author: Weixiang Sun et.al.
2605.12243.authors: Weixiang Sun, Shang Ma, Yiyang Li, Tianyi Ma, Zehong Wang, Colby Nelson, Xusheng Xiao, Yanfang Ye
